title: Can we mutate Info Boxes using the API?
excerpt:
Hello, 
Using the V2 API, is it possible to add items and text in the “Info Boxes” tab within a specific item ? 
Can’t seem to find any doc on this topic. 
Thanks, 
Denis

posts.author: denishacquin (Denis Hacquin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Using the V2 API, is it possible to add items and text in the “Info Boxes” tab within a specific item ?

Can’t seem to find any doc on this topic.

Thanks,

Denis

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey there,

At the moment the info boxes are not accessible via the API. If you’re looking to add text to a particular item, I’d suggest using the Updates section for that! Check it out: Updates Documentation

posts.author: KeriArzaluz (Keri Arzaluz)
posts.cookedHtml: Now that it is official that API v1 will stop working, is there a date to make Info Boxes available in v2?

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Can you tell me more about how you’re using info boxes? We currently don’t have specific plans to add Info Boxes to our API.

Overall, we recommend users move their work from info boxes to the updates section.

The updates section is a much more flexible way to communicate and take notes in your monday.com account. Here are some things you can do with Updates:

  • Encourage collaboration through replies and @mentions
  • Make your writing more clear with additional formatting options
  • Add files and text in the same update (instead of relying on separate info boxes)

Anyway, I’d love to know more about what your workflow is so we can find a solution.

posts.author: KeriArzaluz (Keri Arzaluz)
posts.cookedHtml: We use every pulse/item to represent a specific project. All of our projects have a specific set of links, literature/references that we currently put in the Info Boxes section. We put them there because we actually use the update section a lot for conversations, but there are some documents/links that we need to have more accessible (and all of these come from another system that we have, we connected them through the API). We tried using the update section and pinning the important updates to the top but it is just not as practical because you have to scroll down a lot to find the latest update, you can’t compress the comments to scroll quicker, and adding these documents/links into a column would make our board even more complex.

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Keri, thanks for that explanation!

It sounds like a part of what you need is a better way to sort through a cluttered updates section, as well as a way to add organization so certain kinds of updates stay separate.

I’ll pass this along as feedback to our product team.

posts.author: KeriArzaluz (Keri Arzaluz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @dipro, do you know if it will be possible to mutate the Updates?
I know you can create updates but can you modify an existing update?
I couldn’t find how to in the documentation.

I’ve noticed that the APIv1 is not working properly to change the infoboxes anymore so I am wondering if there’s a way I can move this info to the updates even if they are not easy to see or scroll.

posts.author: zlanich (Zach Lanich)
posts.cookedHtml:
As someone who uses Monday very heavily for a team of creatives, I can say that the Updates section definitely is not a good replacement for Info Boxes for the following reasons:

  1. You can only update your own Updates, making it impossible to use Updates for reference material, specifications, etc, as other people can’t collaboratively add to that material.
  2. The Updates section is a threaded conversation area, and without a re-work of that, reference material would get buried, and we would need a way to categorize that material to keep it organized so we can locate what we’re looking for, both visually and through the API.

I really hope you guys expose Info Boxes to the API, or find an acceptable alternative that allows people to collaboratively update reference material within an item. This is 100% required for our entire workflow, and it would be a huge deal breaker for us if we had nowhere to put that information.

posts.author: assafa (asaf shtrul)
posts.cookedHtml:
HI
Any updates regarding the funcctionality of mutating info boxes? files and notes ?
(I know the updates section can hold large text + files as well… )

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @assafa!

The info boxes have been deprecated.

Regarding files, what do you want to do with them? Are you talking about files in the files column?

Regarding notes, what are you referring to by “note” and what do you want to do with them?
